trivia_bullets.text: Modern Horizons 3 was released June 14, 2024, as a direct-to-Modern draft innovation set. MH3 revisited roughly 40 returning mechanics including Exert (originally from Amonkhet, 2017) and Adapt (originally from Guilds of Ravnica, 2018). Hydra Trainer combines both on a single card — exert for the pump trigger, adapt to self-modify [Scryfall set data; Wizards MH3 Mechanics article; WebSearch MH3 returning mechanics coverage].

Hydra Trainer is a 1/1 for {1}{G} whose exert pump scales with the total number of counters across all permanents you control — not just +1/+1 counters — making it a strong payoff in artifact-counter and energy-counter builds. The card was previewed on May 24, 2024 by @MagicNewGirl on Twitter/X [Scryfall preview source field]. EDHREC rank 11441 indicates narrow Commander adoption [Scryfall EDHREC rank].
sources: Dexerto: Hydra Trainer gives killer boost to counter decks

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Ryan Pancoast, a prolific MTG artist known for lush naturalistic fantasy work across multiple sets. The type line Human Warrior (not Human Druid or Human Ranger) signals the active, combat-focused framing of her bond — she directs the hydra in battle rather than tending it from afar [Scryfall type_line].

Exert was introduced in Amonkhet (2017) with the flavor of citizens pushed to their physical limits by the god-Pharaoh Nicol Bolas. Its reappearance in MH3 strips the theological servitude flavor and recontextualizes the mechanic as trained mastery — a trainer pushing herself and her creatures to peak performance [Wizards Hour of Devastation Mechanics article, magic.wizards.com].
